description: Phase 1 Foundation — Tooling, launch site database, atmospheric profile data, and updated UI. Washington State is the high-fidelity testbed; global coverage is broad but lighter-weight.

context.stack: Next.js 16 + Turso (Drizzle ORM) + shadcn/ui + Leaflet + Tailwind v4
context.weatherAPI: Open-Meteo (free, no API key). Pressure-level params: temperature_XhPa, windspeed_XhPa, winddirection_XhPa, relativehumidity_XhPa, geopotential_height_XhPa, cloudcover_XhPa where X = 1000, 950, 900, 850, 800, 700, 600, 500
context.siteDataAPI: ParaglidingEarth bounding-box API: GET https://www.paragliding.earth/api/getBoundingBoxSites.php?north=X&south=X&east=X&west=X&limit=100&style=detailled — returns XML, no API key needed

userStories.title: Code Quality Tooling
userStories.description: Set up Prettier, Vitest, and Playwright. Add lint-staged + husky for pre-commit hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: Prettier installed and configured (.prettierrc with singleQuote, trailingComma all, printWidth 100)
.prettierignore excludes build outputs, node_modules, drizzle migrations
All existing source files formatted with prettier
Vitest installed with path aliases matching tsconfig (@/* -> ./src/*)
At least one example unit test passing
Playwright installed with chromium, basic smoke test (homepage loads)
Husky + lint-staged configured: pre-commit runs eslint --fix + prettier --write on staged .ts/.tsx/.js files
npm scripts added: test, test:e2e, format, format:check
All existing ESLint errors in the codebase are FIXED (not suppressed) before committing
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes with zero errors
npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: IMPORTANT: The existing codebase has lint errors (any types, unescaped quotes, unused vars, Math.random in render). Fix ALL of them as part of this story. Do not commit until lint is clean.

userStories.title: Launch Site Database Schema
userStories.description: Create new database tables for global launch sites (from ParaglidingEarth) separate from user-created custom sites. Users can favorite launch sites and add personal custom monitoring points.
acceptanceCriteria: launch_sites table created with: id, name, slug (unique), country_code, region, latitude (real), longitude (real), elevation, landing_elevation, orientations (json), site_type, flying_types (json), source, source_id, description, landing_lat, landing_lng, landing_description, max_wind_speed, ideal_wind_directions (json), last_synced_at, created_at, updated_at
user_favorite_sites table created with: id, user_id (FK), site_id (FK to launch_sites), notes, custom_max_wind, custom_ideal_directions (json), notify (boolean), created_at. UNIQUE(user_id, site_id)
custom_sites table created with: id, user_id (FK), name, latitude, longitude, elevation, max_wind_speed, ideal_wind_directions (json), created_at, updated_at
Drizzle migration generated and applies cleanly
Existing user sites data preserved (migrated to custom_sites)
Old sites table kept for backward compat during transition OR cleanly migrated
forecasts_cache updated to work with both launch_sites and custom_sites
TypeScript types exported for all new tables
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Be careful with the migration — existing users have sites with forecasts. Don't lose data. The forecasts_cache FK currently points to sites.id. Consider making it a simple text field (no FK) that can reference either table, or add a site_type discriminator.

userStories.title: ParaglidingEarth Site Scraper
userStories.description: Build a scraper to import paragliding launch sites from ParaglidingEarth's API. Washington State first, global capability built in.
acceptanceCriteria: Scraper service at src/lib/scraper/paraglidingearth.ts
Parses XML response correctly (use a lightweight XML parser like fast-xml-parser)
Maps ParaglidingEarth data to launch_sites schema including orientations, flying types, landing info
Derives ideal_wind_directions array from orientations (directions rated 2=good become ideal)
CLI script at scripts/import-sites.ts runnable via npx tsx scripts/import-sites.ts
Supports --region washington (pre-defined bbox) and --bbox north,south,east,west
Upserts on source_id to avoid duplicates on re-import
Rate limiting: max 1 request per second to ParaglidingEarth
Washington State import succeeds and imports 50+ sites
Unit tests for XML parsing and orientation-to-direction conversion
npm run build succeeds
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: ParaglidingEarth XML format: <search><takeoff><name/><lat/><lng/><takeoff_altitude/><orientations><N>0|1|2</N>...</orientations><paragliding>1</paragliding><hanggliding>0</hanggliding>...</takeoff><landing>...</landing></search>. Rate limit politely. Generate a slug from the name (lowercase, hyphens, no special chars).

userStories.title: Pressure-Level Atmospheric Data Service
userStories.description: Upgrade the weather service to fetch full vertical atmospheric profiles from Open-Meteo at multiple pressure levels, enabling windgram-style visualizations in Phase 2.
acceptanceCriteria: New function fetchAtmosphericProfile(lat, lng, days?) in src/lib/weather-profile.ts
Fetches pressure-level data at 1000, 950, 900, 850, 800, 700, 600, 500 hPa
At each level: temperature, wind_speed, wind_direction, relative_humidity, geopotential_height, cloud_cover

Surface data: temperature_2m, wind_speed_10m, wind_direction_10m, wind_gusts_10m, cloud_cover, cape, precipitation_probability, boundary_layer_height, convective_inhibition, lifted_index, shortwave_radiation
TypeScript types defined: AtmosphericProfile, AtmosphericHour, PressureLevel, SurfaceData
Computed derived parameters: lapse rate between levels (°C/1000ft), thermal index, estimated cloud base (from surface temp/dewpoint spread), estimated top of lift
Separate cache table (atmospheric_profiles_cache) in Turso with 3-hour TTL
API route: GET /api/weather/profile?lat=X&lng=Y&days=2 returns JSON AtmosphericProfile
Existing simple forecast (weather.ts, scoring.ts) still works unchanged
Unit tests for lapse rate calculation, thermal index, cloud base estimation
npm run build succeeds
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: Open-Meteo param format: temperature_1000hPa (not temperature_1000hpa). The geopotential_height gives actual altitude at each pressure level. Lapse rate = (T_lower - T_upper) / (height_upper - height_lower) * 304.8 to get °C/1000ft. Dry adiabatic lapse rate is ~3°C/1000ft. Keep existing weather.ts untouched — this is a NEW service alongside it.

userStories.title: Updated Site Pages & Navigation
userStories.description: Update the UI to browse imported launch sites, favorite them, and see enriched data. Keep existing custom site functionality working.
acceptanceCriteria: Browse Sites page (/sites/browse) showing all imported WA launch sites on a Leaflet map
Site markers on map, clicking opens popup with name + elevation + link to detail page
Search/filter by name and region on browse page
Site detail page (/sites/[slug]) shows enriched data: name, elevation, orientations, landing info, source attribution
Authenticated users can favorite/unfavorite launch sites
Dashboard shows user's favorited launch sites + custom sites
Existing custom site create/edit/delete still works
Mobile-friendly layout (responsive)
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Use slugs for launch site URLs (not UUIDs). Keep the existing /sites page for user's own sites and favorites. The /sites/browse page is for discovery. Leaflet must be dynamically imported (no SSR) — use the existing map-display-wrapper.tsx pattern.

userStories.title: API Routes for Site & Atmospheric Data
userStories.description: Create clean REST API endpoints for sites and atmospheric profiles that the Phase 2 windgram component will consume.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/sites?region=washington&type=paragliding — returns filtered launch sites
GET /api/sites/[slug] — returns single site with full details
GET /api/sites/[slug]/profile — returns atmospheric profile for a specific site
GET /api/weather/profile?lat=X&lng=Y&days=2 — returns atmospheric profile for arbitrary location
All routes return proper JSON with appropriate HTTP status codes
Cache-Control headers set appropriately (atmospheric data: max-age=3600)
Error responses are consistent: { error: string, status: number }
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: The /api/weather/profile route was created in US-004. This story adds the site-specific routes and ensures consistent API design across all endpoints. Consider rate limiting for the profile endpoints since they hit Open-Meteo.
